projects.name: Dashboard Redesign
projects.goal: Rebuild analytics dashboard with real-time data and modern UX to improve user engagement

tech_rationale.React: team expertise and Shadcn UI component library compatibility
tech_rationale.Shadcn UI: accessible, customizable component library matching design system
tech_rationale.Django Channels: WebSocket support for real-time dashboard updates
tech_rationale.GraphQL: flexible querying for dashboard widgets with varying data needs

projects.outcomes: 35% engagement improvement, 60% reduction in page load time, 4.2/5 user satisfaction score

projects.name: API Migration
projects.goal: Migrate internal services from REST to GraphQL for flexible querying and reduced over-fetching

tech_rationale.GraphQL: reduces over-fetching and enables client-driven queries
tech_rationale.Apollo Server: mature ecosystem with caching and federation support
tech_rationale.Swagger: documentation for legacy REST endpoints during transition

projects.name: Payment Gateway Refactor
projects.goal: Replace monolithic payment processor with event-driven architecture for reliability and PCI compliance

tech_rationale.Kafka: async event streaming for payment events with guaranteed delivery
tech_rationale.PostgreSQL: ACID transactions for payment state management
tech_rationale.Stripe: PCI-compliant payment processing with webhook integration
tech_rationale.Python: team expertise and existing library ecosystem

projects.name: Observability Initiative
projects.goal: Implement distributed tracing and unified monitoring across all microservices

tech_rationale.Jaeger: distributed tracing with service dependency visualization
tech_rationale.Datadog: unified dashboards and alerting across infrastructure

projects.name: Mobile App V2
projects.goal: Major mobile app update with offline support, push notifications, and redesigned navigation

tech_rationale.React Native: code sharing with web frontend team
tech_rationale.Firebase: push notifications and real-time database sync
tech_rationale.Expo: faster development iteration and OTA updates

projects.name: ML Recommendation Engine
projects.goal: Build personalized recommendation system to increase user engagement and conversion

tech_rationale.PyTorch: flexible model architecture for recommendation algorithms
tech_rationale.FastAPI: low-latency serving endpoint for real-time predictions
tech_rationale.Redis: feature caching for sub-50ms prediction latency

projects.name: Data Lake Migration
projects.goal: Consolidate disparate data stores into unified analytics warehouse

tech_rationale.Snowflake: scalable cloud warehouse with separation of compute and storage
tech_rationale.Airflow: orchestration for complex ETL dependency graphs
tech_rationale.Spark: large-scale data processing for historical backfill

projects.outcomes: 80% reduction in query times, unified data catalog serving 50+ analysts, $15K/month cost savings from decommissioned legacy systems

projects.name: Auth Service Modernization
projects.goal: Replace legacy session-based auth with OAuth2+JWT for all services

tech_rationale.Vault: secrets management and dynamic credential rotation
tech_rationale.Go: high-performance auth service with low memory footprint
tech_rationale.JWT: stateless token verification reducing database lookups

projects.outcomes: Eliminated session store bottleneck, reduced auth latency from 120ms to 8ms, passed SOC 2 compliance audit

projects.name: Developer Portal
projects.goal: Build self-service developer portal with interactive API docs and onboarding guides

tech_rationale.Next.js: SSR for SEO and fast initial page loads
tech_rationale.MDX: documentation as code with interactive components
tech_rationale.Swagger: auto-generated API reference from OpenAPI specs

projects.outcomes: Reduced partner onboarding from 2 weeks to 3 days, 15 external partners migrated to new docs

projects.name: Service Mesh Rollout
projects.goal: Implement service mesh for traffic management, security, and observability across microservices

tech_rationale.Istio: mature service mesh with mTLS and traffic policies
tech_rationale.Envoy: high-performance sidecar proxy with rich L7 features
tech_rationale.Kubernetes: existing orchestration platform for sidecar injection
tech_rationale.Prometheus: native metrics export from Envoy sidecars

projects.name: Internal Tools Consolidation
projects.goal: Unify 6 internal admin tools into single React application

tech_rationale.Next.js: server components for admin data-heavy pages
tech_rationale.Tailwind CSS: rapid UI development with design system tokens

projects.cancellation_reason: Deprioritized in favor of Mobile App V2 push — team bandwidth insufficient for both. Partial work (shared component library) merged into main design system.

projects.name: Event Sourcing Migration
projects.goal: Migrate core domain services to event sourcing for auditability and replay capability

tech_rationale.Kafka: durable event log with replay capability
tech_rationale.PostgreSQL: event store with JSONB for flexible event schemas

decisions.detail: Adopted Snowflake as primary data warehouse
decisions.rationale: Need scalable analytics warehouse that separates compute from storage to handle growing data volumes cost-effectively

decisions.detail: Chose Apollo Server over Hasura for GraphQL
decisions.rationale: Apollo provides more control over resolver logic and integrates better with existing Django auth middleware

decisions.detail: Selected React over Vue for Dashboard Redesign
decisions.rationale: Existing team expertise in React and compatibility with Shadcn UI component library
Reduce merge conflicts and enable continuous deployment by keeping branches short-lived
Most mature option with built-in mTLS, traffic splitting, and Kubernetes-native integration
Need horizontally scalable SQL database for payment service growth projections exceeding single-node PostgreSQL limits
Integrates with Datadog alerts and provides escalation policies needed for 24/7 coverage

decisions.detail: Standardized on dbt for data transformations
decisions.rationale: Version-controlled SQL transformations with built-in testing reduce data quality issues

decisions.detail: Chose event sourcing over CQRS-only for core domain services
decisions.rationale: Full event log provides auditability for payment compliance and enables temporal queries for debugging

decisions.detail: Selected MLflow over Weights & Biases for experiment tracking
decisions.rationale: Open-source, self-hosted option avoids vendor lock-in and integrates with existing Airflow pipelines

incidents.root_cause: Kafka consumer group rebalance during deployment caused message processing to stall, triggering cascading timeouts in the payment pipeline
incidents.resolution: Rolled back deployment, implemented graceful consumer shutdown and staggered rebalance protocol

incidents.detail: Redis cluster failover — 15 min elevated latency
incidents.root_cause: Primary Redis node ran out of memory due to unbounded cache growth in the recommendation feature cache, triggering automatic failover to replica
incidents.resolution: Set maxmemory-policy to allkeys-lru, added Datadog memory alerts at 80% threshold, documented cache eviction strategy

incidents.detail: GraphQL gateway timeout — 30 min degraded API responses
incidents.root_cause: N+1 query in new Apollo Server resolver caused database connection pool exhaustion under load
incidents.resolution: Added DataLoader batching for the affected resolver, implemented connection pool monitoring

incidents.detail: Airflow scheduler crash — 2 hour data pipeline delay
incidents.root_cause: Airflow scheduler process exhausted file descriptors due to excessive DAG file parsing in the data lake migration DAGs
incidents.resolution: Increased file descriptor limits, consolidated DAG definitions, added health check alerting for scheduler process

events.detail: Opened headcount for 2 senior backend engineers
Full rollout complete, OAuth2 + JWT in production
Staged rollout to production, 35% engagement improvement

events.topic: ML recommendation model A/B test results

events.detail: Launched to all external partners, onboarding time reduced from 2 weeks to 3 days
All data sources migrated to Snowflake, legacy systems decommissioned

adrs.title: Use Kafka for payment event streaming
adrs.author: John Doe
adrs.context: The July payment outage revealed that synchronous payment processing creates cascading failures. We need an async event-driven architecture for payment state transitions.
adrs.decision: Adopt Kafka as the event backbone for all payment-related state changes, with PostgreSQL as the transactional store
adrs.rationale: Kafka provides guaranteed delivery, replay capability, and natural backpressure — critical for payment reliability
alternatives_considered: RabbitMQ — simpler but lacks replay and partitioned ordering
AWS SQS — managed but no log semantics for audit trail
Redis Streams — insufficient durability guarantees for payment data

adrs.title: Adopt Istio service mesh over Linkerd
adrs.author: Ryan O'Brien
adrs.context: With 40+ microservices, we need centralized traffic management, mTLS, and observability. The October service mesh planning meeting identified Istio and Linkerd as finalists.
adrs.decision: Deploy Istio as our service mesh with Envoy sidecars across all Kubernetes namespaces
adrs.rationale: Istio offers richer traffic policies, built-in fault injection for chaos testing, and better Prometheus integration
alternatives_considered: Linkerd — lighter weight but fewer traffic management features
Consul Connect — requires HashiCorp ecosystem buy-in
No mesh — continue with manual mTLS and service discovery
consequences: Higher memory overhead per pod (~50MB sidecar)
Requires Istio expertise on infrastructure team
Enables canary deployments and circuit breaking

adrs.title: Standardize on PagerDuty for incident management
adrs.author: Derek Washington
adrs.context: On-call rotation has been managed via a shared Google Sheet, leading to missed pages and unclear escalation paths. The payment and Redis incidents highlighted the need for proper tooling.
adrs.decision: Adopt PagerDuty for all on-call scheduling, alert routing, and incident escalation
adrs.rationale: PagerDuty integrates natively with Datadog and provides mobile push, SMS, and phone escalation required for 24/7 coverage
alternatives_considered: Opsgenie — cheaper but weaker Datadog integration
VictorOps — being sunset in favor of Splunk On-Call
Custom Slack bot — insufficient for compliance requirements
consequences: Need to migrate existing Datadog alert rules to PagerDuty services
Enables SLA tracking and incident analytics

adrs.title: Use DataLoader pattern for GraphQL N+1 prevention
adrs.author: Priya Patel
adrs.context: The January GraphQL gateway timeout was caused by an N+1 query pattern in a new resolver. We need a systematic approach to prevent this class of bug.
adrs.decision: Mandate DataLoader for all database-accessing GraphQL resolvers, with automated N+1 detection in CI
adrs.rationale: DataLoader batches and caches database calls within a single request, eliminating N+1 queries by design
alternatives_considered: Manual query optimization per resolver — doesn't scale
Hasura-style auto-generated resolvers — too restrictive for our use case
Query complexity limits only — treats symptoms not cause
consequences: Need to train team on DataLoader patterns
Prevents entire class of performance regressions

adrs.title: Select MLflow over Weights & Biases for experiment tracking
adrs.author: Emily Zhang
adrs.context: The ML Recommendation Engine needs experiment tracking for model development. Team evaluated self-hosted vs SaaS options.
adrs.decision: Deploy self-hosted MLflow for experiment tracking, model registry, and artifact storage
adrs.rationale: Open-source avoids vendor lock-in, integrates with existing Airflow pipelines, and keeps experiment data on our infrastructure
alternatives_considered: Weights & Biases — better UX but SaaS-only with data residency concerns
Neptune.ai — good collaboration features but expensive at scale
ClearML — open-source but less mature model registry
consequences: Self-service model deployment through MLflow model registry

adrs.title: Adopt event sourcing for payment domain
adrs.author: John Doe
adrs.context: Payment compliance requirements demand full audit trails. The existing Kafka-based event pipeline (ADR-001) provides the foundation, but we need true event sourcing with aggregate reconstruction.
adrs.decision: Implement event sourcing with Kafka as the event log and PostgreSQL JSONB for event snapshots in the payment domain
adrs.rationale: Full event log satisfies PCI audit requirements and enables temporal debugging of payment state transitions
alternatives_considered: Change data capture from PostgreSQL WAL — loses domain event semantics
CQRS without event sourcing — insufficient for compliance audit trail
consequences: Significant refactoring of payment service write path

adrs.title: Service mesh mTLS rollout strategy
adrs.author: Ryan O'Brien
adrs.context: Istio is deployed but mTLS is in permissive mode. Need a strategy to enforce strict mTLS across all namespaces without breaking existing services.
adrs.decision: Phased namespace-by-namespace mTLS enforcement starting with non-critical services, with automated compliance checking
adrs.rationale: Gradual rollout reduces blast radius of misconfigurations while still achieving full encryption within Q2
alternatives_considered: Big-bang enforcement — too risky for production traffic
Application-level TLS — inconsistent and harder to audit

adrs.title: Feature store architecture for ML serving
adrs.author: Emily Zhang
adrs.context: ML Recommendation Engine serving requires sub-50ms feature retrieval. Current approach of computing features at request time adds 200ms latency.
adrs.decision: Build two-tier feature store: Redis for online serving (<10ms) and Snowflake for offline training batch features
adrs.rationale: Separation of online/offline feature paths optimizes for both latency and compute cost
alternatives_considered: Feast — open-source but adds operational complexity we may not need yet
Single Redis tier — insufficient for large historical feature sets
Pre-computed feature tables in PostgreSQL — too slow for real-time serving
consequences: Redis capacity planning for feature data
Enables real-time personalization across all products
